_comment: One record per line. POST 1000-at-a-time to AI Search /docs/index?api-version=2024-07-01 with @search.action='upload'. content_vector is shown truncated for readability — production payloads carry the full 3072-float array from text-embedding-3-large.

content: Case study: pharma-grade glass vials for an injectable launch > The brief. A clinical-stage biotech needed Type I borosilicate vials that could pass USP 660 chemical resistance and hold a sensitive monoclonal antibody at minus eighty Celsius.
title: Case study: pharma-grade glass vials for an injectable launch

content: Case study: pharma-grade glass vials for an injectable launch > Qualification. We qualified two MJS pharma-grade glass vial SKUs against their fill-finish line, built a custom siliconisation profile for the closures, and supported their regulatory submission with a full extractables and leachables package.
title: Case study: pharma-grade glass vials for an injectable launch

content: Product: borosilicate Type I pharma-grade glass vials > Specification. property: hydrolytic_resistance, value: USP <660> Type I. property: dimensional_tolerance, value: +/- 0.10 mm OD. property: particulate_burden, value: <= 6000 particles >= 10um per container.
title: Product: borosilicate Type I pharma-grade glass vials

content: Why borosilicate Type I is still the default for biologics. The honest answer is that the alternatives still trade at a quality risk we are not willing to take for an injectable program at minus eighty. Glass is well-characterised, the supply chain is mature, and the failure modes are known.
title: Podcast Ep. 21: pharma cold chain packaging with Dr Lena Weiss

content: Annealing oven calibration. We run a controlled cooling profile after forming, and the calibration of this oven directly drives the residual stress in the finished vial. We re-baseline weekly against a thermocouple reference array.
title: Video: a tour of our pharma-grade glass production line
